title: Why did Gendo let Ritsuko live
display_title: Why did Gendo let Ritsuko live
wikitext:
[]: Why did Gendo let Ritsuko live?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sat Sep 03, 2011 8:30 pm This is something that occurred to me a little while ago during a fanfic binge. The scene was more or less an analysis of how crazy Rit-chan was after the Armisael debacle and when she finally snapped. The fallout of this was, of course, her destroying the Rei copies before breaking down sobbing. Now we have a dangerous, emotionally unstable woman who's one of the few people who actually knows the full story about the series, and who just dealt Gendo's plans a near crippling blow. Why on Earth did he let such a liability live?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Sep 03, 2011 8:33 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by John » Sat Sep 03, 2011 8:34 pm She's the only one who can ehmm fully "understand" the complex MAGI System. Gendo could only keep her locked until the plan was completed.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Sat Sep 03, 2011 11:25 pm He still had a use for her.

[]: Re: Why did Gendo let Ritsuko live?             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Sep 03, 2011 11:39 pm CyberXIII wrote: Why on Earth did he let such a liability live?

[]: Why did Gendo let Ritsuko live? Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Sep 04, 2011 12:54 am CyberXIII wrote: Why on Earth did he let such a liability live?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Sep 04, 2011 8:10 am Gendo isn't a psychopath and as a commander has to follow official procedure in the case of such situations. The interment was probably temporary before she was to be put on official trial.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sun Sep 04, 2011 11:38 am Huh. I figured there was a logical reason for it, but the only reason I brought it up in the first place is because of what she pulls in EoE after ostensibly saving everyone.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Sep 04, 2011 12:00 pm Gendo is not omnipotent and didn't know she was going to try and blow everything up. Who knows, maybe he even felt somewhat sympathetic towards her, a la Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by John » Sun Sep 04, 2011 12:13 pm Trajan wrote: Who knows, maybe he even felt somewhat sympathetic towards her, a la Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Sep 04, 2011 12:22 pm Trajan wrote: Who knows, maybe he even felt somewhat sympathetic towards her, a la Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Sun Sep 04, 2011 12:53 pm Allemann wrote: He wouldn't have shot her later on.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Sep 04, 2011 5:17 pm He probably still felt sympathy for her when he shot her, but obviously she'd made her choice to continue to be a hindrance and he couldn't allow that to stop him.

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Sun Sep 04, 2011 5:52 pm If he knew she would still be absorbed during Instrumentality it's possible he didn't think killing her was as final as it initially appears; but then we don't really know exactly what Gendo's plans were or whether he would have expected this to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Sep 05, 2011 2:56 am Trajan wrote: Just because he shot her then, doesn't mean he didn't feel sympathetic (or regretful of his actions) towards her earlier on. Whatever sympathy he may or may not have had towards her was probably eroded away by the fact that she had just tried to kill everyone in the Geo-Front.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Sep 05, 2011 4:04 am Allemann wrote: He shot her after her botched attempt at blowing everything away. She wasn't a threat any longer, but he still shot her. I doubt he ever had genuine sympathy for her. Look at his relationship with Naoko.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Sep 05, 2011 4:39 am NemZ wrote: She's still standing in his way and armed. Given her emotional state it's entirely likely she'd try to kill him and Rei with the pistol given the opportunity.

[]:             Quote Post by chloe00 » Tue Sep 06, 2011 2:43 am Allemann wrote: He shot her after her botched attempt at blowing everything away. She wasn't a threat any longer, but he still shot her. I doubt he ever had genuine sympathy for her. Look at his relationship with Naoko.

[]: Why did Gendo let Ritsuko live?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sat Sep 03, 2011 8:30 pm This is something that occurred to me a little while ago during a fanfic binge. The fallout of this was, of course, her destroying the Rei copies before breaking down sobbing. Now we have a dangerous, emotionally unstable woman who's one of the few people who actually knows the full story about the series, and who just dealt Gendo's plans a near crippling blow. Why on Earth did he let such a liability live?
